action_history.reason: K5s has suited king, GTO indicates 95% call frequency in BB vs open with 3:1 pot odds

T6s is a marginal suited hand with some playability. Checking to see a free flop is optimal - not strong enough to raise for value out of position, but has enough potential to continue postflop if we hit.
T6s completely missed the Q-4-3 flop. Ten-high with no draws. Checking to see opponent's action - will likely fold to any bet with no equity.

Ten-high with no draws on Q-4-3-2 board. Both players showed weakness checking the flop. Checking to see the river - no reason to bluff with zero equity when opponent could easily have any piece of this board.
K5s has flush draw and gutshot straight draw (~13 outs), opponent has shown weakness checking twice, betting for value and protection
